things.name: Lentos Art Museum
things.description: Spectacular glass and steel museum on the Danube riverbank with an outstanding collection of modern and contemporary Austrian art

things.name: Ars Electronica Center
things.description: Pioneering museum of the future exploring digital art, science and technology; Linz's glowing blue landmark on the Danube

things.name: Hauptplatz
things.description: One of Central Europe's largest baroque market squares, dominated by the 1723 Trinity Column and lined with elegant facades

things.name: Linz Cathedral (New Cathedral)
things.description: Austria's largest church with a soaring 134-metre neo-Gothic spire and extraordinary stained-glass windows depicting Linz's history

things.name: Postlingberg Pilgrimage Church
things.description: Twin-towered baroque pilgrimage church perched on a hilltop above the Danube, reached by one of Europe's steepest rack railways

things.name: Linz Castle
things.description: Medieval fortress atop the castle hill houses the Upper Austrian Provincial Museum, with sweeping views over the Danube and city

things.name: OK Contemporary Arts Center
things.description: Leading contemporary arts venue in the historic Ursulinenhof presenting cutting-edge international exhibitions and performances

things.name: Bruckner Orchestra Concert
things.description: Linz's celebrated orchestra named after composer Anton Bruckner; performances at the Brucknerhaus concert hall on the Danube

things.name: Danube Riverfront Promenade
things.description: Vibrant riverside walk linking the Lentos and Ars Electronica buildings, popular for cycling, dining and watching the river traffic

things.name: RAIFFEISEN ARENA
things.description: Home of LASK Linz with a 19,080-seat capacity; Linz's modern football stadium in the southern suburb of Pichling
